_note: KRYSHA lever catalog add-on. New schema fields: actor, legitimacy{band,authority,blowback,sustainability,note}, flips[], case_basis. origin marks existing vs new.

actor_vocabulary.INDUCED_RUSSIAN_SIDE: Russian-side predation the disruptor enables but does not control
actor_vocabulary.OGA: Other Government Agencies — non-LE national-level capabilities (deliberately unspecified)

legitimacy_axis.authority: is the actor empowered; does it burn a case/source
legitimacy_axis.blowback: does it harden the target or escalate (LOW good / HIGH bad)

coalition._note: Who is at the table. The planner starts with `default` only; the analyst adds members to build a coalition. `capacity` is points/quarter that member contributes; a lever is only usable if its `actor` is in the coalition (hard capability gate). Roster is DATA — extend it on an airgapped deployment (add named/real partners) without touching code.

members.name: Western LE
members.note: Your home agency; the backbone. Indictments, seizures, undercover.

members.name: Allied / cooperative LE
members.note: Unlocks arrests where a jurisdiction cooperates (the travel-window plays).

members.name: Regulators (Treasury / OFAC)
members.note: Unlocks sanctions, designations, de-banking. Durable, defensible.

members.name: Private sector / providers
members.note: Hosting, registrars, CDNs, analytics. Fast, deniable, reconstitutes.

members.name: Investigative media
members.note: Deniable public-domain exposure; no Western-state fingerprint.

members.name: Induced Russian-side
members.note: Predation you ENABLE but do not control. Cheap capacity, low legitimacy/authority.

members.name: Other Government Agencies (OGA)
members.note: Non-LE national-level capabilities — offensive cyber, directed-deniable operations, and technical attribution. Broad and powerful; the heaviest operations are escalatory or exposure-sensitive and need authorization. (Deliberately unspecified.)

windows._note: PLANNED openings (deterministic — the analyst or a scenario places them on a quarter). Each grants extra capacity and/or unlocks an actor's levers FOR THAT QUARTER ONLY. This is how Phobos Aetor worked: spot the opening, concentrate force into it. Data — extend on the airgapped box.

types.name: Travel window
types.note: The operator leaves protected territory — arrests in a cooperative jurisdiction become possible this quarter.

types.name: Political crack
types.note: The krysha wavers — Russian-side predation can be induced this quarter.

types.name: Funding / task-force surge
types.note: Extra resources land this quarter — pure capacity, no new partner.

types.name: Media moment
types.note: A leak or story breaks — the press channel opens this quarter.

types.name: OGA authorization (green-light)
types.note: A finding / green-light — enables the heaviest OGA operations (offensive & covert) this quarter. Or flip OGA to always-authorized.

levers.name: Technical Attribution / Unmasking
levers.effect: Technical collection unmasks the operators — anonymous actors become named and located.
levers.teaching_point: Attribution is the precondition for every finish: a sanction or arrest is only as good as the unmasking under it.

legitimacy.note: Lawful collection, high value — but acting on it can burn sources and methods.

levers.name: Offensive C2 Neutralization
levers.effect: Neutralize botnet/loader command-and-control by force.
levers.teaching_point: Offensive takedown is fast and forceful — but it reconstitutes without pairing, and it is escalatory.

legitimacy.note: Authorized offensive operation; effective but escalatory and burns access.

flips.note: Loud offensive action can harden protection / provoke escalation.

levers.case_basis: REvil taken offline by offensive action, 2021.

levers.name: Forced Infrastructure Takedown
levers.effect: Degrade or seize hosting infrastructure by means unavailable to LE or providers.
levers.teaching_point: Reaches infrastructure in non-cooperative space — but infra alone rebuilds, now with escalation cost on top.

levers.name: Offensive Leak-Site Disruption
levers.effect: Force the negotiation/leak site offline by offensive means — the authorized version of the deniable DDoS.
levers.teaching_point: Same effect as the deniable hacktivist play, but with authority behind it and a fingerprint on it.

levers.name: Directed Influence Operation
levers.effect: A controlled, deniable influence operation that fractures trust inside the ecosystem.
levers.teaching_point: The controlled cousin of induced distrust: you direct it — deniable, until it is exposed.

legitimacy.note: Covert and directed; deniable but oversight-sensitive.

flips.note: Exposure of a covert operation collapses its legitimacy.

levers.case_basis: Directed influence; contrast with the uncontrolled Conti fracture.

levers.name: Directed Insider Operation
levers.effect: A controlled insider/penetration operation that surfaces identities and severs protection from within.
levers.teaching_point: The controlled cousin of induced predation: directed and deniable, with the same exposure risk.
levers.case_basis: Contrast with the Satuchin induced (uncontrolled) predation.

levers.name: Designate OTC Broker Cluster
levers.effect: Strands large cash-out; compounds each turn it stays in place.
levers.teaching_point: Financial designation is the most durable vector: infrastructure rebuilds in days, financial exposure compounds.

legitimacy.note: Treasury/OFAC authority is clear and defensible; slow but durable.

levers.name: VASP KYC / Enhanced Due Diligence Push
levers.effect: Narrows compliant cash-out gateways; raises friction.
levers.teaching_point: Each designation raises actor cost even when an alternative exists.

legitimacy.note: Regulatory engagement, not coercion; high authority, low blowback.

levers.name: Designate Mixing Service
levers.effect: Flags mixer flows; raises downstream compliance friction.
levers.teaching_point: Designation degrades fungibility of laundered proceeds.

levers.name: Upstream Provider Engagement (BPH)
levers.effect: Pressures transit/peering to drop the bulletproof host.
levers.teaching_point: Infrastructure pressure works only when paired with a money or identity vector.

legitimacy.note: Private peering pressure is legitimate but reconstitutes fast.

levers.name: Leak-Site Seizure
levers.effect: Seizes the leak/negotiation site; temporary.
levers.teaching_point: Seizure alone is a leadership transition, not degradation.

legitimacy.note: Warrant/MLAT-backed seizure; legitimate but short-lived alone.

levers.case_basis: LockBit relaunched in 5 days; BlackCat leak site back in 48-72h.

levers.name: Seize Site + Release Decryptor (Cronos model)
levers.effect: Seizure plus decryptor and affiliate-data reveal corrodes trust.
levers.teaching_point: Pairing seizure with a trust attack is what made Operation Cronos bite.

legitimacy.note: Cronos-style psyop on top of lawful seizure; high legitimacy and high effect.

levers.name: Sinkhole Loader Botnet (Duck Hunt model)
levers.effect: Sinkholes C2; capacity returns unless paired with money/identity.
levers.teaching_point: A sinkhole alone fails; add the money vector (the Duck Hunt lesson).

legitimacy.note: Lawful court-authorized takeover; reconstitutes without pairing.

levers.name: Infiltrate / Take Down IAB Market
levers.effect: Undercover presence + takedown seeds buyer distrust.
levers.teaching_point: Infiltration degrades trust as well as infrastructure.

legitimacy.note: Undercover market work is well-established and deniable.

levers.name: Stealer-Log Market Takedown
levers.effect: Removes a stealer-log marketplace; supply re-lists elsewhere.
levers.teaching_point: Takedown without victim notification leaves the supply intact.

levers.name: Disrupt Escrow / Arbitrator (Trust Node)
levers.effect: Compromising escrow/arbitration collapses deal-making confidence.
levers.teaching_point: Trust infrastructure is a higher-leverage target than any single market.

legitimacy.note: Hitting escrow is lawful and structurally potent.

levers.name: Third-Country Arrest (travel window)
levers.effect: Arrest during travel to a cooperative jurisdiction removes the operator.
levers.teaching_point: Operators are reachable only when they leave protected territory.

legitimacy.note: Lawful extradition/arrest; depends on a travel window.

levers.name: Public Indictment / Attribution
levers.effect: Names the operator publicly; signals capability.
levers.teaching_point: Going loud before protection is severed hardens the krysha (protection-reflex trap).

legitimacy.note: Lawful, but counterproductive if timed wrong.

flips.note: Hardens protection, burns the element of surprise; legitimacy effectively LOW here.
With protection cut, attribution converts to durable identity pressure.

levers.name: FNS Lifestyle-Inconsistency Referral
levers.effect: Tax-authority referral on unexplained wealth; also pressures protectors.
levers.teaching_point: Domestic tax exposure is a rare lever that reaches protected actors.

legitimacy.note: Uses Russia's own tax apparatus; deniable, low blowback.

levers.name: Mule Recruitment / Front-Company Disruption
levers.effect: Dismantles recruitment funnels and front companies.
levers.teaching_point: Cash-out depends on replaceable but frictional human networks.

levers.name: Officer Financial Exposure Operation
levers.effect: Surfaces a protecting officer's financial footprint.
levers.teaching_point: The protector has personal exposure that the protected do not.

legitimacy.note: Depends on method: sanctions/FOIA-grade = higher; intrusion = lower.

levers.name: Investigative-Journalism Pipeline
levers.effect: Feeds verified material to investigative outlets.
levers.teaching_point: Public-domain exposure via press is deniable and politically durable.

legitimacy.note: Free-press channel; no Western-state fingerprint, hard to retaliate against.

levers.name: Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD) Reform
levers.effect: Shrinks the exploit-broker market by speeding patching.
levers.teaching_point: Reducing exploit shelf-life starves the access pipeline upstream.

levers.name: Correspondent-Banking / Payment-Rail Cutoff
levers.effect: Severs fiat on/off-ramps the broker relies on.
levers.teaching_point: Cutting the fiat rail hurts more than naming the wallet.

legitimacy.note: Correspondent de-risking is standard and durable.

levers.name: Operator Arrest in Cooperative CIS State
levers.effect: Arrest where the broker operates outside Russian protection (KZ/GE/AM).
levers.teaching_point: OTC brokers travel and base in selectively-cooperative states.

legitimacy.note: Lawful where jurisdiction cooperates; not available in Russia.

levers.name: Sanction / Designate Exchange
levers.effect: Designates the non-compliant exchange; cuts it from compliant rails.
levers.teaching_point: Designation strands the cash-out venue, not just a wallet.

levers.name: Correspondent De-Banking
levers.effect: Pressures correspondent banks to drop the exchange.
levers.teaching_point: An exchange with no banking is a wallet with no exit.

levers.name: Host-Country LE Raid / Seizure
levers.effect: Physical/legal seizure where the host is incorporated.
levers.teaching_point: BPH dies only when the host-country acts; upstream pressure alone is temporary.

legitimacy.note: Lawful raid where jurisdiction allows; reconstitutes if not paired.

levers.name: Registrar / IP-Range Deplatform
levers.effect: Revokes domains and IP allocations tied to the host.
levers.teaching_point: Infrastructure ID is replaceable but each rotation costs the actor.

levers.name: Indict Exploit Broker
levers.effect: Charges a named broker selling access to RaaS.
levers.teaching_point: Brokers are individuals with travel exposure.

levers.name: Market Interference / Bug-Bounty Crowd-Out
levers.effect: Legitimate bounties out-compete criminal exploit demand.
levers.teaching_point: Raising the legitimate price of bugs starves the criminal market.

levers.name: Honeypot Access Listings
levers.effect: Sells salted/booby-trapped access to poison the market.
levers.teaching_point: Poisoned supply degrades trust faster than takedown.

legitimacy.note: Undercover deception is lawful and high-leverage.

levers.name: Arrest Market Admin
levers.effect: Removes the market operator via cooperative jurisdiction.
levers.teaching_point: Admin removal collapses confidence if paired with data reveal.

levers.name: C2 Domain / Host Takedown
levers.effect: Kills active C2 via registrar/host action.
levers.teaching_point: C2 takedown is fast but rebuilds without financial pairing.

levers.name: Indict Loader Operator
levers.effect: Names the botnet operator.
levers.teaching_point: Naming without severing protection hardens the krysha.

levers.name: Hosting / CDN Deplatform
levers.effect: Pressures CDN/host to drop the leak site.
levers.teaching_point: Deplatforming is friction, not death.

levers.name: Persistent DDoS of Leak Site
levers.effect: Keeps the negotiation/leak site offline by force.
levers.teaching_point: Offensive disruption is effective but legally gray for state actors.

legitimacy.note: Extralegal for Western LE; better deniably induced. Vigilante/hacktivist territory.

flips.note: Authority problem; do not own it.

levers.name: Reputation / Sockpuppet Attack on Brokers
levers.effect: Seeds accusations of snitching/scamming against key brokers.
levers.teaching_point: Manufactured distrust is cheaper than arrests and self-sustains.

legitimacy.note: Influence operation; deniable, gray on authority.

levers.name: Induce Internal Distrust (rip-and-run signal)
levers.effect: Amplifies the perception that members are cooperating or stealing.
levers.teaching_point: Trust collapse from within is the cheapest path to ecosystem degradation.

legitimacy.note: Induced, not directly controlled; deniable, no Western fingerprint.

levers.name: Seize Mixer Infrastructure
levers.effect: Court-ordered seizure of mixer servers/keys.
levers.teaching_point: Seizure + designation compounds the laundering tax.

levers.name: On-Chain Demixing + Attribution
levers.effect: Tracing that defeats the mixer and attributes flows.
levers.teaching_point: Demonstrated demixing erodes the value proposition of the mixer.

levers.name: Bank Fraud-Control Tightening
levers.effect: Pushes receiving banks to flag mule patterns.
levers.teaching_point: Raising mule attrition raises the whole network's cost.

levers.name: Arrest Laundering Network
levers.effect: Coordinated arrests of the cash-out crew.
levers.teaching_point: Networks are replaceable but each takedown imposes real delay.

levers.name: Seize + Victim Notification
levers.effect: Takedown plus credential reset at scale devalues the logs.
levers.teaching_point: Notifying victims is what turns a takedown into supply destruction.

levers.name: Payment-Processor Cutoff
levers.effect: Cuts the subscription-payment processors the market uses.
levers.teaching_point: Even crypto-first markets have payment chokepoints.

levers.name: Crypter Service Takedown
levers.effect: Removes the crypter/packer service and its panel.
levers.teaching_point: Detection evasion is a service with its own infrastructure.

levers.name: Force AV/EDR Detection of Stub
levers.effect: Industry coordination to burn the crypter's evasion stub.
levers.teaching_point: Burning the stub resets the service's whole value proposition.

levers.name: Indict Crypter Developer
levers.effect: Charges the named developer.
levers.teaching_point: Small dev teams are individually exposed.

levers.name: Provider Abuse-Desk Pressure
levers.effect: Forces resellers to enforce abuse policy.
levers.teaching_point: Gray-market VPS depends on lax abuse handling.

levers.name: Reseller KYC Mandate
levers.effect: Regulatory push for reseller identity checks.
levers.teaching_point: KYC at the reseller raises the cost of anonymous infrastructure.

levers.name: Registrar Suspension at Scale
levers.effect: Bulk suspension of malicious domains.
levers.teaching_point: Domain churn is cheap; sustained registrar pressure compounds.

levers.name: DNS Firewall / RPZ Blocking
levers.effect: Resolver-level blocking of malicious domains.
levers.teaching_point: Protective resolution shrinks reach without a takedown.

levers.name: Seize Exfil Staging Infrastructure
levers.effect: Removes cloud/staging buckets used for data theft.
levers.teaching_point: Cutting staging interrupts the double-extortion pipeline.

levers.name: Cloud-Provider Monitoring + Disruption
levers.effect: Provider-side detection of bulk-exfil patterns.
levers.teaching_point: The same providers staging the data can detect it.

levers.name: Residential-Proxy Provider Pressure
levers.effect: Pressures proxy providers sourcing criminal traffic.
levers.teaching_point: Anonymization layers have commercial chokepoints.

levers.name: Infiltrate + Attribute Proxy Network
levers.effect: Undercover mapping that strips anonymity.
levers.teaching_point: Mapping the proxy layer enables attribution upstream.

levers.name: Public Doxxing of Operator by LE
levers.effect: State publicly publishes the operator's real identity/personal data.
levers.teaching_point: LE-attributed doxxing is the textbook protection-reflex trap: it hardens the krysha and burns collection.

legitimacy.note: Extrajudicial naming sits outside clean LE authority, signals sources/methods, and consolidates state protection.

flips.note: Worst case: hardens protection, no durable pressure.
Marginally better once protection is gone, but a formal indictment is the cleaner instrument.

levers.case_basis: Contrast with Conti/Trickbot deanonymization that worked via leaks, not LE press releases.

levers.name: Induce Internal Predation (fake-krysha / insider leak)
levers.effect: Create conditions for Russian-side actors to prey on the operators (fake-siloviki extortion, amplified insider leaks).
levers.teaching_point: When deanonymization surfaces through Russian-side predation, it imposes identity/trust cost with no Western fingerprint and no authority problem.

legitimacy.note: High effect, deniable, politically clean for the West, but not directly controllable and conditionally good.

flips.note: Predation can harden the strong while culling the weak.
May mislead targeting; treat WAIS impact as ANALYST_INFERENCE.

levers.case_basis: Ruslan Satuchin fake-FSB extortion of Conti (charged Art.159 ch.4, 2025).

levers.name: Rewards-for-Justice Bounty
levers.effect: Public bounty incentivizes insiders to inform.
levers.teaching_point: A bounty monetizes the distrust that already exists inside the crew.

legitimacy.note: Lawful incentive; can also spook the crew into security.

levers.name: Sanction Named Operator
levers.effect: OFAC designation of the individual operator.
levers.teaching_point: Personal sanctions constrain travel and cash-out simultaneously.
levers.case_basis: OFAC designations of LockBitSupp / Conti members.

levers.name: Sanction / Name the Protector
levers.effect: Designates or names the protecting officer/unit.
levers.teaching_point: Sanctioning the protector raises the cost of providing krysha.

levers.name: Diplomatic / Visa Pressure on Patrons
levers.effect: Targets the patrons' families/assets abroad via visa and asset measures.
levers.teaching_point: Protectors have offshore exposure the protected lack.
